suggestions: I'm grateful for the rezeki of a stable tech job where I can build meaningful things every day.
I'm thankful for a warm glass of teh tarik and a quick laugh with colleagues during our afternoon break.
I'm grateful for the quiet moments of peace right after Fajr before the hustle of the day begins.
I'm thankful for that incredibly satisfying feeling when a complex bug is finally resolved and the pipeline turns green.
I'm grateful for the cool morning rain that made my commute to the office feel calm and refreshing.
I'm thankful for the blessing of heading home to a warm, home-cooked meal with my family.
I'm grateful for a smooth, conflict-free Git merge on a Friday afternoon.
I'm thankful for the chance to step away from my dual monitors and find stillness during my daily prayers.
I'm grateful for finding an empty seat on the LRT during the busy evening rush hour.
I'm thankful for patient senior developers who take the time to review my pull requests and help me grow.
I'm grateful for the simple joy of hot, crispy curry puffs and a fresh brew from the local kopitiam.
I'm thankful for the weekly reset and the sense of community I feel during Friday prayers.
I'm grateful for a workplace culture that respects my time, allowing me to log off and unwind properly.
I'm thankful for the beautiful sunset over the city skyline after a long day of coding.
I'm grateful for those rare, magical moments when my code runs perfectly on the very first try.
